findings.summary: Removed the repeated contact-page closing CTA block so call/directions/menu remain the main path without repeating the same hours pitch three times.
Made the shared hours table stack on small screens to reduce cramped wrapping risk for long public-hours strings.
Fresh desktop/mobile homepage/contact screenshots were captured from the local Next build in the parent host environment after the worker sandbox capture path was blocked.
Added an explicit Next viewport export so mobile devices render at device width and QA screenshots no longer use a desktop-width layout viewport.

fixes: Removed `ContactCtaClosing` from the contact page to reduce duplication and confusion.
Made the contact-page CTAs stack cleanly on mobile and added a direct menu-highlights link.
Changed `HoursTable` to stack rows on small screens and right-align only on larger breakpoints.
Added `export const viewport` in `app/layout.tsx` with `width: device-width` and `initialScale: 1`.

screenshot_status.method: Parent host Chrome headless against local Next server on 127.0.0.1:3026

remaining_blockers.message: MC auth is unavailable in the runtime, so the prepared qa_round_1 payload could not be synced.
Public sources still conflict on hours, so QA keeps the call-to-confirm language in place.
Public ordering signals exist, but a direct owner-controlled ordering provider was not verified.

notes: QA round 1 focused on practical sell-readiness: first-view clarity, mobile readability, CTA hierarchy, contact-page flow, truth safety, and concierge guardrails.
No unsupported claims were added during QA fixes.
Local stage is advanced to qa_round_1 with fresh screenshot evidence captured from the local Next build.
